summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV attacks across several fronts, including Chernihiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia regions. Russian‑linked incidents included drone strikes on a refinery in Tyumen and road explosions in LNR. Civilian casualties were confirmed in Kharkiv and Kyiv regions.
key_points: Ukrainian UAVs were launched from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk, continuing operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).
A UAV strike hit a two‑storey apartment building in Kharkiv's Holodnogirskiy district, killing one and injuring nine (Source: @UkraineNow/82064).
Russian drones attacked Kyiv region warehouses in Boryspil district, causing a fire and hospitalising a 40‑year‑old man (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Two guided aerial bombs were deployed over Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/65691).
Ukrainian forces dropped explosive devices on a LNR road, killing two people and restricting movement near the Dzholzansky checkpoint (Source: @tass_agency/381588).
Russian forces struck the Tyumen oil refinery 2000 km from Ukraine, reporting at least two explosions (Source: @UkraineNow/82074).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over multiple locations in Chernihiv, including Makoshyne, Olishivka to Otrohy, and Kulykivka to Korop (Source: @eRadarrua/97445, @eRadarrua/97429).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near Velyka Oleksandrivka in Kherson region, heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/65678).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV operations intensified across northern and eastern fronts, targeting Russian‑held areas and logistics. Russian forces responded with drone strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure, including a distant refinery and road attacks in LNR. Civilian casualties rose in Kharkiv and Kyiv regions, indicating heightened risk to non‑combatants.

points: Ukrainian UAVs launched from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk continued missions, indicating cross‑border strike capability (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).

In Chernihiv region, UAV clusters were reported over Makoshyne, Olishivka to Otrohy, and from Kulykivka to Korop, showing sustained aerial pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/97413, @eRadarrua/97429, @eRadarrua/97420).
Dnipropetrovsk saw two guided UAV attacks near Synelnykove and Pavlohrad, suggesting use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/65691, @eRadarrua/97445).

Kharkiv region experienced UAV activity near Slatyne and Bohodukhiv heading toward Kharkiv, and a strike on a two‑storey apartment building causing one death and nine injuries (Source: @kpszsu/65701, @kpszsu/65709, @UkraineNow/82064).
Zaporizhzhia reported UAVs moving north toward Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia, indicating pressure on the northern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65696).
Ukrainian forces dropped explosive devices on a LNR road, killing two and limiting traffic near Dzholzansky checkpoint (Source: @tass_agency/381588).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs were observed over Trostyanets in Sumy, potentially serving as targeting guides (Source: @kpszsu/65707).
Russian forces launched UAVs from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk, sustaining offensive air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).
A drone attack on the Tyumen oil refinery, 2000 km from Ukraine, caused at least two explosions and emergency pressure release (Source: @UkraineNow/82074).
Explosive devices were deployed on a LNR road, resulting in two civilian deaths and road closures (Source: @tass_agency/381588).
Russian defense ministry reported shooting down multiple UAVs near Moscow and in the Bryansk region, including an incident injuring an 11‑year‑old child in Trubchevsk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian forces claimed destruction of Ukrainian naval ships in the Zaporozhye maritime corridor and a railway echelon in Kharkiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
In the Krasnaya Liman area, Russian reports indicated counter‑attacks and logistical challenges due to destroyed crossings (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian authorities reported a knife attack in a Krasnodar shopping centre, unrelated to the front but reflecting internal security incidents (Source: @tass_agency/381606).
Ukrainian UAVs operated near Makoshyne and Synelnykove in the Chernihiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, targeting eastern supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/97413, @kpszsu/65691).
Kharkiv saw UAVs passing near Slatyne and Bohodukhiv toward the city, with a strike on a residential building causing civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/65701, @kpszsu/65709, @UkraineNow/82064).
In Sumy, reconnaissance UAVs were detected over Trostyanets, potentially guiding further attacks (Source: @kpszsu/65707).
Russian reports indicated UAV attacks on the city of Trubchevsk in Bryansk, injuring a child, showing cross‑border activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian forces reported progress of several hundred meters in Bachivsk and ongoing battles in Ivolzhansk and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian claims of destroying a Ukrainian railway echelon in Kharkiv suggest targeting of logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted near the village of Kachiy Lopan' on the Kharkiv front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs launched from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk indicate northern operational reach (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).
Ukrainian guided aerial bombs were used in Dnipropetrovsk, showing north‑south strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65691).
Russian UAV attacks were reported near Moscow, with 76 drones shot down near the capital (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
In the Bryansk region, a UAV attacked Trubchevsk, injuring an 11‑year‑old child (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity near Kyiv, with alerts for potential air defense engagement (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67917).
Russian reports of UAV interceptions near Moscow suggest heightened air defense activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the northern Zaporizhzhia sector heading toward Dnipro (Source: @kpszsu/65696).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Velyka Oleksandrivka in Kherson, heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/65678).
Russian drone strikes hit Kyiv region warehouses in Boryspil district, causing fire and civilian injury (Source: @UkraineNow/82075).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV attacks in the Kherson region, injuring two civilians in Behtery (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian claims of destroying two naval ships in the Zaporozhye maritime corridor indicate southern naval engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted over the southern Zaporizhzhia area, targeting logistics routes (Source: @kpszsu/65696).
Russian reports of attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Donetsk region, including injuries on the Donetsk‑NovoaZovsk highway (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near the village of Behtery, causing civilian injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAV launches from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk continued, showing sustained air offensive (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).
Russian UAV attacks on Tyumen refinery and LNR road illustrate long‑range strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/82074, @tass_agency/381588).
Ukrainian guided aerial bombs were deployed in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating precision strike use (Source: @kpszsu/65691).
Russian air defense shot down 76 drones near Moscow and intercepted UAVs in Bryansk, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs caused civilian casualties in Kharkiv and Kyiv regions, highlighting the risk of collateral damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82064, @UkraineNow/82075).
Russian reports of destroying Ukrainian logistics assets, including a railway echelon in Kharkiv, show targeting of supply lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs over Trostyanets suggest intelligence‑gathering role in the air war (Source: @kpszsu/65707).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs launched from Orla, Bryansk and Kursk continue cross‑border strike operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97402).
A UAV strike on a Kharkiv apartment building killed one civilian and injured nine, indicating urban vulnerability (Source: @UkraineNow/82064).
Russian drones attacked the Tyumen oil refinery 2000 km from Ukraine, causing explosions and emergency shutdowns (Source: @UkraineNow/82074).
Explosive devices placed on a LNR road killed two civilians and forced checkpoint closures (Source: @tass_agency/381588).
Ukrainian guided aerial bombs were used in Dnipropetrovsk, showing precision strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/65691).
Russian air defenses shot down 76 drones near Moscow and intercepted UAVs in Bryansk, reflecting heightened aerial defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).

Ukrainian UAV activity reported across multiple fronts, including Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson, indicating broad operational reach (Source: @eRadarrua/97413, @kpszsu/65701, @kpszsu/65678).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations are expanding both in number and geographic scope, targeting Russian logistics and border areas, while Russian long‑range drone strikes demonstrate the ability to hit strategic infrastructure far from the front. Civilian casualties in urban centers raise the risk of escalated domestic backlash. Both sides are intensifying air defense measures, as evidenced by high UAV shoot‑down rates near Moscow and in contested zones.
sections.regional_response: Poland has withdrawn the Order of the White Eagle from President Zelensky, reflecting political friction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31036). Hungary continues to block certain EU accession steps for Ukraine while lifting media bans, indicating mixed regional support (Source: @DeepStateUA/23587). Russian internal security incidents, such as the Krasnodar shopping‑centre attack, may divert attention from front‑line operations (Source: @tass_agency/381606).
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian UAV launch sites in Russia suggest coordination with cross‑border assets, requiring monitoring of airspace violations. The use of guided aerial bombs points to increased precision strike capability, potentially affecting Russian supply lines. Russian claims of destroying Ukrainian naval assets in the Zaporozhye corridor warrant verification. Continued civilian casualties underscore the need for improved target discrimination and protective measures.
